title: Portability Across Applications
concept: Why one MCP server works unmodified across many different host applications, unlike bespoke function-calling glue.

exercises.prompt: What did we learn in the last lesson about the primary benefits MCP adds on top of basic function calling?

options.text: It forces LLMs to run locally without internet access.
It provides standardized discovery, reusable servers, and structured resources.
It replaces Python with a brand-new programming language.

exercises.explain: MCP standardizes discovery, resources, and prompts, allowing servers to be reused across different applications rather than being locked to one bespoke implementation.

cards.title: The Integration Nightmare
cards.body: If you build a great helper tool for your LLM, do you want to rewrite it for every new chat app, IDE, or terminal tool that comes out? MCP solves this fragmentation forever.

cards.title: What is Portability?
cards.body: Portability means a tool or service can run in different environments without being rewritten. With MCP, you write the server once, and any host application that supports MCP can immediately use its tools.

cards.title: The USB-C of AI Tools
cards.body: Think of a USB-C cable. Instead of needing a different, custom charger for every brand of phone, you use one standard cable that plugs into everything. MCP acts like USB-C for AI tools.

cards.title: Write Once, Run Anywhere
cards.body: Imagine you write a database search tool. In the old way, you wrote custom integration code for VS Code and different code for your Slack bot. With MCP, you run one MCP server, and both VS Code and Slack connect to it directly.

cards.title: No LLM-Specific Code Needed
cards.body: Many think you need to write special adapter code for different LLMs (like OpenAI vs Anthropic) on the server side. In reality, the host application handles the LLM translation; your MCP server just talks standard MCP.

cards.title: Bespoke vs. Portable
cards.body: Traditional tools require custom wrappers for every app. MCP uses a single standard interface to connect to everything.

left.text: Messy spaghetti code mapping tools to App A, App B, and App C.

right.text: One clean server plugged into any host application instantly.

exercises.prompt: What makes traditional, bespoke function calling difficult to port to new host applications?

options.text: It is automatically converted by the operating system.
It requires writing custom 'glue code' for each application's unique API format.
It uses a single global standard that all apps natively share.
It only works inside web browsers and nowhere else.

exercises.explain: Bespoke function calling requires you to write custom integration code ('glue code') to map your tools to each specific application's API.

exercises.prompt: True or False: An MCP server written to fetch weather data can be used by both a command-line chat tool and a desktop IDE GUI without changing the server's code.
exercises.explain: MCP defines a universal interface, meaning the exact same server code can be connected to any supporting client application.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to describe how an MCP server runs across different host applications:
exercises.sentence: An MCP server runs unmodified across different hosts because of standard communication protocols.
exercises.explain: Because MCP uses a universal protocol, the server code remains completely unmodified, saving developers from writing repetitive glue code.

exercises.prompt: Which of these descriptions of how MCP portability works sounds like it was generated by a confused AI?

options.text: The host app establishes a session with the MCP server, queries its list of available tools, and maps them to the model's schema dynamically.
The MCP server compiles its tools into a proprietary binary format that must be uploaded to the official LLM App Store before execution.
The server exposes tools via standard JSON-RPC, meaning any host client can read the schema and call the tools without local code changes.

exercises.explain: Option B showcases a typical AI hallucination or over-complicated setup. MCP tools are dynamically discovered at runtime over a simple standard protocol, not packaged into proprietary app store binaries.

exercises.prompt: Why can the exact same MCP server run on Claude Desktop, a terminal assistant, and a custom web app without rewriting a single line of server code?

options.text: You must write custom API adapter code for each new host application you want to support.
The host application dynamically discovers the server's tools over a standard JSON-RPC protocol.
The host application translates the MCP protocol into hardcoded Python functions first.
MCP requires compiling the server code into a different executable for every LLM provider.

exercises.explain: Because MCP standardizes the communication protocol, any compliant host client can immediately discover and run the tools exposed by an MCP server without needing code modifications. Standardized JSON-RPC replaces custom application-level API wrappers.
